params.description: View the git diff for the latest commit

final_text:
```json
[
  {
    "title": "Authorization header not stripped on cross-domain redirects (credential leak)",
    "category": "security",
    "severity": "critical",
    "confidence": 0.99,
    "file": "requests/sessions.py",
    "start_line": 289,
    "end_line": 289,
    "evidence": [
      "The diff removes the check: if \"Authorization\" in headers and self.should_strip_auth(response.request.url, url): del headers[\"Authorization\"]",
      "rebuild_auth() is called on every redirect at line 246: self.rebuild_auth(prepared_request, resp)",
      "should_strip_auth() still exists at line 127 and correctly detects cross-host, scheme-downgrade, and port-change redirects, but is now dead code in this context",
      "After the patch, rebuild_auth() only applies netrc auth but never removes the existing Authorization header"
    ],
    "risk": "When a server redirects a request to a different host (e.g., attacker-controlled domain), the Authorization header (Bearer tokens, Basic credentials) is forwarded to the new host, leaking credentials. This is a classic open-redirect credential theft vector. Any application using requests sessions with authentication that follows redirects is vulnerable.",
    "suggested_fix": "Restore the removed lines in rebuild_auth(): before the netrc check, add back `if \"Authorization\" in headers and self.should_strip_auth(response.request.url, url): del headers[\"Authorization\"]`"
  }
]
```
